metadata.project: Full Steam Ahead — Course Cover Images
metadata.notes: All images must match the established 2A1 series style exactly. See style_constants below.

style_constants.background: Dark navy-blue industrial scene, deep charcoal and navy tones (#0d1f35 to #1a3a5c), subtle blueprint grid lines visible across the entire image, dramatic cinematic industrial lighting
style_constants.operator_attire: Dark navy/charcoal full-length coverall worksuit with bright orange hi-vis panels on shoulders and chest, silver reflective safety tape stripes on arms and torso, dark hard hat, clear safety glasses. Photorealistic, professional appearance.
style_constants.headline_style: Top-left of image. Very large, bold, condensed sans-serif uppercase font. 'SECOND CLASS' in solid white. 'POWER ENGINEERING — [PAPER ID]' in bright orange (#E8590C). Second line below in smaller bold uppercase white/light gray: the paper subtitle topics.
style_constants.hologram_style: Blueprint-style cyan and white technical engineering diagrams floating as semi-transparent holographic overlays in the mid-ground, glowing faintly
style_constants.icon_strip: Bottom 18% of image: dark navy rounded rectangular panel with 4 evenly-spaced small technical illustrations in orange (#E8590C) and white line-art style on dark background
style_constants.overall_quality: Photorealistic, cinematic, 4K quality, dramatic depth of field with background slightly defocused, warm amber/orange industrial glow from operating equipment, professional color grading

images.prompt:
Photorealistic cinematic course cover image for a professional Canadian power engineering exam prep course. 16:9 landscape format.

BACKGROUND (left 55%): A large industrial heat exchanger network — shell-and-tube exchangers, pressure vessels, and steam headers stretching into the distance inside a power plant. Warm amber and orange glow from active steam flow. Slightly defocused for depth. Dark navy and charcoal tones with subtle blueprint grid lines overlaid across the full image. Semi-transparent holographic overlays floating in the mid-ground: a Temperature-entropy (T-s) diagram showing the Rankine cycle, and a heat exchanger schematic with labeled components — all in glowing cyan and white blueprint style.

OPERATOR (right 40%): A female power engineer standing on the RIGHT side of the frame, facing left toward the equipment. She is standing confidently with her arms crossed and a professional smile — she is NOT holding a tablet or clipboard. She wears: dark navy/charcoal coverall worksuit with bright orange hi-vis shoulder and chest panels, silver reflective safety tape stripes, dark hard hat, clear safety glasses. Photorealistic, mid-30s, professional and authoritative.

TEXT OVERLAID ON IMAGE — TOP LEFT: Line 1 (very large, bold, condensed uppercase): 'SECOND CLASS' in white followed by 'POWER ENGINEERING — 2A2' in bright orange (#E8590C). Line 2 (smaller bold uppercase): 'THERMODYNAMICS  –  METALLURGY  –  MATERIALS' in light gray/white.

BOTTOM ICON STRIP: Dark navy rounded panel across the bottom 18% of image. Four evenly-spaced small technical illustrations in orange and white line-art: (1) Bell curve / Carnot efficiency graph, (2) Crucible pouring molten metal, (3) Crystal lattice / atomic structure, (4) Tensile test specimen with stress-strain curve.

Overall: Dramatic cinematic depth of field, warm industrial amber lighting from equipment, professional color grading, dark navy and charcoal palette with orange accents. Photorealistic 4K quality.

images.headline_line2: BOILERS & STEAM GENERATORS  –  PUMPS  –  WATER TREATMENT
images.prompt:
Photorealistic cinematic course cover image for a professional Canadian power engineering exam prep course. 16:9 landscape format.

BACKGROUND (right 55%): A massive industrial fire-tube boiler interior — large cylindrical shell with rows of fire tubes visible in cross-section, furnace firebox glowing orange, steam drum and mud drum connections, safety valves on top. Industrial water treatment tanks and clarifiers visible further in background. Warm amber and orange glow from the furnace. Slightly defocused. Dark navy and charcoal tones with subtle blueprint grid lines overlaid across the full image. Semi-transparent holographic overlays floating in mid-ground: a labeled cutaway boiler diagram showing tube arrangement, steam drum, and circulation path — glowing cyan and white blueprint style.

OPERATOR (left 40%): A male power engineer standing on the LEFT side of the frame, facing right toward the equipment. He is performing a hands-on inspection — leaning slightly forward, holding a clipboard and pen in one hand, closely examining a valve or gauge on nearby equipment with focused attention. He wears: dark navy/charcoal coverall worksuit with bright orange hi-vis shoulder and chest panels, silver reflective safety tape stripes, dark hard hat, clear safety glasses. Photorealistic, mid-40s, experienced and focused.

TEXT OVERLAID ON IMAGE — TOP LEFT: Line 1 (very large, bold, condensed uppercase): 'SECOND CLASS' in white followed by 'POWER ENGINEERING — 2A3' in bright orange (#E8590C). Line 2 (smaller bold uppercase): 'BOILERS & STEAM GENERATORS  –  PUMPS  –  WATER TREATMENT' in light gray/white.

BOTTOM ICON STRIP: Dark navy rounded panel across the bottom 18% of image. Four evenly-spaced small technical illustrations in orange and white line-art: (1) Fire-tube boiler schematic cross-section with tubes visible, (2) Centrifugal pump cutaway showing impeller, (3) Water droplet with filter media column, (4) Ion exchange / water softener column schematic.

Overall: Dramatic cinematic depth of field, warm industrial amber and orange lighting from boiler furnace, professional color grading, dark navy and charcoal palette with orange accents. Photorealistic 4K quality.

images.headline_line2: HEAT ENGINES & PRIME MOVERS  –  LUBRICATION  –  PIPING  –  DRAWING
images.prompt:
Photorealistic cinematic course cover image for a professional Canadian power engineering exam prep course. 16:9 landscape format.

BACKGROUND (right 55%): A large industrial reciprocating steam engine or diesel prime mover — exposed pistons, connecting rods, crankshaft, and flywheel visible with dramatic mechanical detail. Industrial piping manifolds, flanged connections, and valve assemblies in the background. Warm amber and orange glow from the running machinery. Slightly defocused. Dark navy and charcoal tones with subtle blueprint grid lines overlaid across the full image. Semi-transparent holographic overlays floating in mid-ground: a Pressure-Volume (P-V) indicator diagram for an engine cycle, and an isometric piping schematic with labeled fittings — glowing cyan and white blueprint style.

OPERATOR (left 40%): A female power engineer standing on the LEFT side of the frame. She is pointing confidently at a large technical engineering blueprint/drawing mounted or held up beside her, explaining or reviewing it — engaged, professional, mid-gesture. She is NOT holding a tablet. She wears: dark navy/charcoal coverall worksuit with bright orange hi-vis shoulder and chest panels, silver reflective safety tape stripes, dark hard hat, clear safety glasses. Photorealistic, late 20s to mid-30s, engaged and knowledgeable.

TEXT OVERLAID ON IMAGE — TOP LEFT: Line 1 (very large, bold, condensed uppercase): 'SECOND CLASS' in white followed by 'POWER ENGINEERING — 2B1' in bright orange (#E8590C). Line 2 (smaller bold uppercase): 'HEAT ENGINES & PRIME MOVERS  –  LUBRICATION  –  PIPING  –  DRAWING' in light gray/white.

BOTTOM ICON STRIP: Dark navy rounded panel across the bottom 18% of image. Four evenly-spaced small technical illustrations in orange and white line-art: (1) Piston-cylinder engine cross-section with connecting rod, (2) Oil lubrication circuit schematic with pump and filter, (3) Isometric pipe elbow and flange fitting, (4) Orthographic three-view engineering drawing symbol (first/third angle projection symbol).

Overall: Dramatic cinematic depth of field, warm industrial amber lighting from machinery, professional color grading, dark navy and charcoal palette with orange accents. Photorealistic 4K quality.

images.headline_line2: PLANT SYSTEMS  –  INSTRUMENTATION  –  FUELS & COMBUSTION  –  ENVIRONMENT
images.prompt:
Photorealistic cinematic course cover image for a professional Canadian power engineering exam prep course. 16:9 landscape format.

BACKGROUND (left 55%): An industrial control room viewed from inside — large DCS (Distributed Control System) operator screens showing plant process graphics, instrument panels with gauges and indicators, and through a window behind the screens a combustion furnace glowing bright orange. Annunciator panels with indicator lights. Slightly defocused for depth. Dark navy and charcoal tones with subtle blueprint grid lines overlaid across the full image. Semi-transparent holographic overlays floating in mid-ground: a P&ID (Piping and Instrumentation Diagram) schematic with control loop symbols, and a combustion triangle (fuel-air-ignition) diagram — glowing cyan and white blueprint style.

OPERATOR (right 40%): A male power engineer standing on the RIGHT side of the frame, facing left. He is standing upright, arms confidently crossed over his chest, with a relaxed professional smile — calm and authoritative. He is NOT holding anything. He wears: dark navy/charcoal coverall worksuit with bright orange hi-vis shoulder and chest panels, silver reflective safety tape stripes, dark hard hat, clear safety glasses. Photorealistic, mid-40s to early 50s, experienced and confident.

TEXT OVERLAID ON IMAGE — TOP LEFT: Line 1 (very large, bold, condensed uppercase): 'SECOND CLASS' in white followed by 'POWER ENGINEERING — 2B2' in bright orange (#E8590C). Line 2 (smaller bold uppercase): 'PLANT SYSTEMS  –  INSTRUMENTATION  –  FUELS & COMBUSTION  –  ENVIRONMENT' in light gray/white.

BOTTOM ICON STRIP: Dark navy rounded panel across the bottom 18% of image. Four evenly-spaced small technical illustrations in orange and white line-art: (1) PID controller feedback loop diagram with set point and process variable, (2) Flame / combustion burner schematic, (3) Plant process flow block diagram, (4) Leaf or recycling arrows symbol representing environmental compliance.

Overall: Dramatic cinematic depth of field, warm orange glow from furnace and instrument displays, professional color grading, dark navy and charcoal palette with orange accents. Photorealistic 4K quality.

Photorealistic cinematic course cover image for a professional Canadian power engineering exam prep course. 16:9 landscape format.

BACKGROUND (right 55%): A large industrial air compressor unit — reciprocating or screw type, with intercooler vessels, pressure piping, and safety valves — alongside high-voltage electrical switchgear panels with glowing indicator lights, bus bars, and circuit breakers. Cool blue-white electrical glow mixed with warm amber from the compressor. Slightly defocused for depth. Dark navy and charcoal tones with subtle blueprint grid lines overlaid across the full image. Semi-transparent holographic overlays floating in mid-ground: a refrigeration cycle pressure-enthalpy (P-h) diagram with labeled evaporator/condenser/expansion points, and a three-phase electrical sine wave diagram — glowing cyan and white blueprint style.

OPERATOR (left 40%): A female power engineer standing on the LEFT side of the frame, facing right toward the electrical panel. She is actively working — holding a digital multimeter in one hand, probing a terminal or measurement point on an electrical panel or instrument with focused professional attention. She wears: dark navy/charcoal coverall worksuit with bright orange hi-vis shoulder and chest panels, silver reflective safety tape stripes, dark hard hat, clear safety glasses. Photorealistic, early 30s, focused and technically competent.

TEXT OVERLAID ON IMAGE — TOP LEFT: Line 1 (very large, bold, condensed uppercase): 'SECOND CLASS' in white followed by 'POWER ENGINEERING — 2B3' in bright orange (#E8590C). Line 2 (smaller bold uppercase): 'ELECTROTECHNOLOGY  –  AIR & GAS COMPRESSION  –  REFRIGERATION' in light gray/white.

BOTTOM ICON STRIP: Dark navy rounded panel across the bottom 18% of image. Four evenly-spaced small technical illustrations in orange and white line-art: (1) Three-phase induction motor cross-section diagram, (2) Reciprocating compressor cross-section showing piston and valves, (3) Refrigeration cycle schematic with compressor-condenser-expansion valve-evaporator loop, (4) Three-phase AC sine waves (R-Y-B).

Overall: Dramatic cinematic depth of field, cool blue-white electrical glow mixed with warm amber, professional color grading, dark navy and charcoal palette with orange accents. Photorealistic 4K quality.
